attacking_style: Launches long-range attacks 10km+ from finish on climbs, relying on diesel engine capacity to sustain efforts. Prone to overextending when attacking too early, particularly beyond 10km threshold.
team_tactics: Often operates in breakaways with minimal team support, showing willingness to work cooperatively with rivals. National/Italian coordination sometimes supersedes team orders in tactical decisions.
terrain_preference: Medium-mountain stages with sustained climbs allowing diesel-paced efforts. Exceptional descending skills enable him to bridge back after being dropped on climbs (30+ second gaps closed).
vulnerability: Lacks explosive acceleration needed for final sprint finishes after hard efforts. Struggles to match late-race attacks from altitude-trained rivals. Timing errors lead to premature cracking when attacking beyond optimal range.
key_patterns: Initiates solo or small-group attacks 10km from finish on climbs (2019 Giro win, 2018 attempts)
Superior descending ability compensates for climbing deficits, bridges 30s+ gaps (2021 Lombardia)
Strong breakaway commitment but lacks final sprint punch after sustained efforts (2020 Tirreno twice)
Tactical timing errors: cracks when attacking too early beyond 10km threshold (2018 Giro, 2.8km from finish)
Local course knowledge advantage in Bergamo region enhances tactical execution (2021 Lombardia)
form_trajectory: neutral: Recent evidence shows strong positioning and descending but lacks stage-winning punch seen in 2019 Giro breakthrough
